body.body:
From Akien 2026-04-15: the shape of an Igor memory is id + three key/value stacks (metadata, triggers, payload), not a wide row with typed columns. This is the target architecture after the many-small-tables split from the current single 'memories' table. Anything that needs to live with a memory (creation date, kind, TTL, test_data flag, etc.) goes into metadata — not into a new column.

Claude hit the wall of this principle when scoping T-test-data-lifecycle: reached for 'expires_at column' before catching himself. The rule is implied by D260 (payload) and DP13 in subsystem_memory.dsb but is not written as a principle anywhere Igor or CC could find it by grep or palace_read.

## Scope

1. Add `memory_node_shape` to `architecture_root.dsb` CORE_PRINCIPLES, alongside `everything_is_memory` and `timestamp_id_format_uniform`. One line, references subsystem_memory.dsb and D260 for detail.
2. Add palace node `theigors/architecture/principles/memory-node-shape` mirroring the CORE_PRINCIPLE content — same pattern as the other 11 principle palace nodes already there.
3. Optional: add a DP entry in subsystem_memory.dsb DESIGN_POINTS section explicitly stating 'memory node shape is id + metadata + triggers + payload; no new columns for per-memory fields'.
4. docs_sync + palace_sync so repo echo is up to date.

## Why S size

10-15 minutes of documentation work. Mirrors the T-future-self-proud-principle sprint shape from earlier today. No code changes, no tests needed (it's a principle doc).

## Content (draft)

'memory_node_shape=a memory is id + three key/value stacks (metadata, triggers, payload) — not a row of typed columns. Kind-of-memory lives in metadata, not in a column. Triggers point into payload. Payload carries data and simple code (engram programs — see D260). Anything that needs to live with a memory (creation date, TTL, test_data flag, employer_id, scope) goes into metadata. Don''t add columns for per-memory state; the wide-table shape is a migration artifact being split into many small tables.'

## Provenance

Akien 2026-04-15 during T-test-data-lifecycle scoping. The test_data/TTL ticket triggered the principle being surfaced. future_self_proud_test applies: documenting this now prevents future-me + future-Igor from the same reach-for-a-column reflex.
body.title: Document memory-node shape (id + metadata + triggers + payload, not columns) as CORE_PRINCIPLE
body.result: 12th CORE_PRINCIPLE in architecture_root.dsb + DP14 in subsystem_memory.dsb + palace node. Documents id + 3 k/v stacks (metadata, triggers, payload) as the canonical shape — no new columns for per-memory state.
